labels.complianceDesc: Our platform is kept updated with the latest changes from Receita Federal. All calculations follow the official equivalence tables and municipal VAU variations.
labels.logicLockedDesc: The breakdown of our tax-saving methodologies is exclusive to clients with an active Strategic Planning service.
labels.strategyLogicDesc: Instead of paying one large amount at the end based on a government estimate (VAU), this strategy uses real monthly payroll records to document labor costs. This path lets you deduct monthly contributions already paid from the final debt, resulting in massive savings.
labels.validationLinksDesc: Legal foundations and official resources that validate this calculation method.

proposal.legalNote: Application of the adjustment factor benefit per IN 10.2021/21. We provide legal backing and fiscal responsibility. If the project period shifts, total values remain the same for fees and taxes; construction INSS is recalculated monthly and may have minor adjustments.
proposal.title: INSS Construction Tax Reduction Advisory Proposal
proposal.validity: Proposal valid for 10 days from the send date.

scenarios.installmentsNote: Includes SELIC interest. No extra principal reduction.
scenarios.monthlyNote: Strategic monthly planning can significantly reduce your tax.
scenarios.standardDesc: Pay as a lump sum after construction
scenarios.standardNote: Uses government VAU estimation. Limited strategic reductions.

strategySteps.step1Desc: The project is registered in the National Registry of Works (CNO) to create a unique tax ID.
strategySteps.step2Desc: All workers and service providers are linked monthly to the project ID via eSocial.
strategySteps.step3Desc: Monthly tax returns are sent to the Federal Revenue to record paid contributions as credits.
strategySteps.step4Desc: Upon completion, the system clears the 'documented' labor against the 'estimated' requirement, resulting in a reduced final DARF.

inssPlanning.subtitle: Compare different payment strategies to optimize your construction tax liability.
inssPlanning.title: INSS Planning

helpContent.aboutDebtText:
Construction INSS is calculated in the **DCTFWEB Aferição de obras** within **SERO**. After submitting the **DCTFWeb**, the taxpayer has until the **20th** of the following month to pay the calculated amount in full, **without additional charges**, through the **DARF**.

If the 20th is not a business day, the due date is moved earlier. The taxpayer chooses whether to pay in full or in installments.
helpContent.autoDebitText: The installment plan is only effective if a bank account is provided. This account must belong to the person responsible for the construction. The account must be from a standard commercial bank, never from digital banks.
helpContent.chargesText: The value of each installment is increased by interest equivalent to the monthly accumulated Selic rate plus **1%** in the month of payment. There may also be penalties and interest on the total amount in case of late payment.
helpContent.dueDatesText: The first installment will be due on the day the installment plan is requested. The others will be due on the last business day of each month.
helpContent.fillingText: When filling in the client's bank details, pay attention to the rules established by the RFB. There is a PDF in the installments menu. Read this document carefully.
helpContent.firstInstallmentText: For the installment plan to take effect, it is necessary to pay the first installment via a DARF issued by the system. The remaining installments will be automatically debited from the provided bank account.
helpContent.installmentCountText: Installments can be made in up to **60 times**. Minimum installments of **R$200** for individuals and **R$500** for legal entities.
helpContent.penaltyText:
The original amount is increased by a **20%** penalty, regardless of the number of installments.

**Example:** INSS in full: **R$10,000.00**. In installments: **R$12,000.00**
helpContent.whenText: INSS debt calculated after submitting the **DCTFWEB** Aferição de obras can be paid in installments after **7 days past due**.
